requirement_text: The HLA implementation shall be scoped as a framework for interoperating federates, federations, object models, and RTI-supported information exchange.
notes: Repo documentation captures the framework boundary and product-set scope, but this framework-wide statement remains broader than the current executable runtime evidence.

requirement_text: The implementation shall preserve HLA's purpose of interoperability and reuse by separating federate behavior, federation rules, RTI services, and OMT object-model description.
notes: The repo documents the separation between framework rules, service semantics, and OMT structure, but the imported purpose statement remains broader than the current direct verification slices.

requirement_text: Project documentation shall treat IEEE 1516, IEEE 1516.1, and IEEE 1516.2 as an interrelated product set and maintain traceability across them.
notes: Current repo documentation and hierarchy artifacts explicitly treat IEEE 1516, 1516.1, and 1516.2 as one interrelated product set with cross-standard traceability.

requirement_text: The object model implementation shall distinguish HLA object classes/object instances from programming-language classes/objects.
notes: The repo clearly models HLA object and interaction structures independently from Python class semantics, but this cross-domain conceptual statement is still broader than the current direct proof surface.

requirement_text: A federation shall identify the FOM as the contract for all HLA data exchanged during federation execution.
notes: The current OMT/FOM/MIM parser, merge, and federation-creation evidence directly supports the framework FOM contract and its OMT-based documentation shape.

requirement_text: The federation FOM shall be documented using one or more FOM modules and one MOM and Initialization Module.
notes: The current OMT/FOM/MIM parser, merge, and federation-creation evidence directly supports the framework FOM contract and its OMT-based documentation shape.

requirement_text: The FOM shall document the agreement among federates about exchanged data and minimum exchange conditions.
notes: The current OMT/FOM/MIM parser, merge, and federation-creation evidence directly supports the framework FOM contract and its OMT-based documentation shape.

requirement_text: A FOM shall be documented in the OMT format prescribed by IEEE 1516.2-2010.
notes: The current OMT/FOM/MIM parser, merge, and federation-creation evidence directly supports the framework FOM contract and its OMT-based documentation shape.

requirement_text: Each federation shall have an HLA Federation Object Model documented in accordance with the OMT.
notes: Current repo-native FOM and MIM handling directly satisfies the Rule 1 requirement that each federation operate with an HLA FOM documented via the OMT.

requirement_text: Federations shall define a FOM documented using the HLA OMT.
notes: The current OMT/FOM/MIM parser, merge, and federation-creation evidence directly supports the framework FOM contract and its OMT-based documentation shape.

requirement_text: Simulation-associated object instance attribute values shall reside in joined federates rather than in the RTI.
notes: Ownership and MOM evidence show the intended split between federate-owned simulation state and RTI-owned management state, but the imported framework rule is broader than the current focused proofs.

requirement_text: Joined federate-associated instance attributes shall be owned by federates, except MOM attributes owned by the RTI.
notes: Ownership and MOM evidence show the intended split between federate-owned simulation state and RTI-owned management state, but the imported framework rule is broader than the current focused proofs.

requirement_text: The RTI may maintain data needed to support services, but shall not be the authoritative simulation-state owner.
notes: Ownership and MOM evidence show the intended split between federate-owned simulation state and RTI-owned management state, but the imported framework rule is broader than the current focused proofs.

requirement_text: Object instance representation for simulation-associated state shall reside in federates rather than the RTI except for standard management data.
notes: Current ownership and MOM slices support the architectural direction of Rule 2, but not every implied state-model consequence is directly isolated today.

requirement_text: Simulation-associated object instance state shall reside in federates rather than the RTI, except standard MOM-managed data.
notes: Ownership and MOM evidence show the intended split between federate-owned simulation state and RTI-owned management state, but the imported framework rule is broader than the current focused proofs.

requirement_text: FOM data exchanged among joined federates during execution shall pass through RTI services.
notes: The repo directly proves RTI-mediated exchange paths and declaration-driven routing, but the imported framework statements also include broader federate-responsibility language that remains only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: Joined federates shall declare the information they provide and require before runtime exchange.
notes: The repo directly proves RTI-mediated exchange paths and declaration-driven routing, but the imported framework statements also include broader federate-responsibility language that remains only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: The RTI shall coordinate, synchronize, and exchange data according to the FOM and federate declarations.
notes: The repo directly proves RTI-mediated exchange paths and declaration-driven routing, but the imported framework statements also include broader federate-responsibility language that remains only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: Federates shall remain responsible for substantive correctness of produced and consumed data.
notes: The repo directly proves RTI-mediated exchange paths and declaration-driven routing, but the imported framework statements also include broader federate-responsibility language that remains only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: Federates shall exchange FOM-defined data through RTI services during federation execution.
notes: RTI-mediated exchange is directly exercised, but this framework rule remains broader than the current clause-level exchange witnesses.

requirement_text: During execution, exchange of FOM-defined data among joined federates shall occur through RTI services.
notes: The repo directly proves RTI-mediated exchange paths and declaration-driven routing, but the imported framework statements also include broader federate-responsibility language that remains only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: Joined federates shall use the standard HLA federate interface to access RTI services.
notes: The standard federate interface surface is strongly represented by signature audits, but the imported framework statements cover the whole IEEE 1516.1 behavioral contract and remain broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: RTI/federate interaction behavior shall conform to IEEE 1516.1-2010 interface semantics.
notes: The standard federate interface surface is strongly represented by signature audits, but the imported framework statements cover the whole IEEE 1516.1 behavioral contract and remain broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: Federates shall interact with the RTI using the standard HLA federate interface.
notes: Current API-surface audits prove substantial standard-interface coverage, but Rule 4 remains broader than the present clause-by-clause executable witnesses.

requirement_text: Joined federates shall interact with the RTI through the standard HLA federate interface.
notes: The standard federate interface surface is strongly represented by signature audits, but the imported framework statements cover the whole IEEE 1516.1 behavioral contract and remain broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: At most one joined federate shall own a given instance attribute at any point in time.
notes: Current ownership-management runtime and callback evidence directly covers single-owner semantics and dynamic acquisition/divestiture support.

requirement_text: The RTI shall support dynamic acquisition and divestiture of instance-attribute ownership during execution.
notes: Current ownership-management runtime and callback evidence directly covers single-owner semantics and dynamic acquisition/divestiture support.

requirement_text: At most one federate shall own a given object instance attribute at a given time.
notes: The repo directly proves the Rule 5 single-owner ownership constraint for object instance attributes.

requirement_text: An object instance attribute shall be owned by at most one joined federate at a time.
notes: Current ownership-management runtime and callback evidence directly covers single-owner semantics and dynamic acquisition/divestiture support.

requirement_text: A federate shall have an HLA SOM documented in accordance with the HLA OMT.
notes: The repo parses and preserves SOM example artifacts in OMT form, but the framework-wide claim that each federate carries a complete SOM is broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: A SOM shall describe object classes, attributes, and interaction classes that the federate can make public.
notes: The repo parses and preserves SOM example artifacts in OMT form, but the framework-wide claim that each federate carries a complete SOM is broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: A SOM shall be specified using one or more SOM modules and may include a MIM.
notes: The repo parses and preserves SOM example artifacts in OMT form, but the framework-wide claim that each federate carries a complete SOM is broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: Each federate shall have an HLA Simulation Object Model documented in accordance with the OMT.
notes: Current SOM parsing evidence supports the OMT-documented SOM concept, but the framework rule remains broader than the repo’s current federate-specific documentation proofs.

requirement_text: Federates shall define a SOM documented using the HLA OMT.
notes: The repo parses and preserves SOM example artifacts in OMT form, but the framework-wide claim that each federate carries a complete SOM is broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: A federate shall be able to update or reflect attributes as specified in its SOM.
notes: Publish/subscribe and update/interaction runtime behavior is covered, but the SOM capability statement remains broader than the current direct per-federate documentation proof.

requirement_text: A federate shall be able to send or receive interactions as specified in its SOM.
notes: Publish/subscribe and update/interaction runtime behavior is covered, but the SOM capability statement remains broader than the current direct per-federate documentation proof.

requirement_text: Externally visible data exchange capabilities shall be documented in the federate SOM.
notes: Publish/subscribe and update/interaction runtime behavior is covered, but the SOM capability statement remains broader than the current direct per-federate documentation proof.

requirement_text: Federates shall be able to publish and subscribe to object attributes and interactions consistently with their SOM and the federation FOM.
notes: Declaration and object-management runtime tests support the publish/subscribe rule, but the imported framework row also depends on SOM/FOM consistency that is only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: Federates shall support the update
current_status: or receipt capabilities described in their SOMs.
notes: partial
column16: Current clause-level publication and subscription proofs support this capability statement, but not all SOM-facing obligations are isolated directly.

requirement_text: A federate shall be able to transfer or accept ownership dynamically as specified in its SOM.
notes: Dynamic ownership transfer flows are directly exercised, but the broader SOM-description framing of the framework row remains only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: A SOM shall document attributes whose ownership may be acquired or divested during execution.
notes: Dynamic ownership transfer flows are directly exercised, but the broader SOM-description framing of the framework row remains only partially harmonized.

requirement_text: Federates shall be able to transfer and accept ownership of object instance attributes dynamically according to HLA ownership-management rules.
notes: Ownership-management runtime behavior is covered, but the rule’s full framework phrasing is broader than the present direct proof set.

requirement_text: Federates shall support dynamic ownership transfer or acceptance as described in their SOMs.
notes: Dynamic ownership transfer support exists, but the federate-SOM framing remains broader than the current isolated evidence.

requirement_text: A federate shall be able to vary update conditions for instance attributes as specified in its SOM.
notes: The repo preserves and exposes update-rate metadata and time-state helpers, but broader update-condition semantics remain only partially implemented and harmonized.

requirement_text: A SOM shall document update conditions such as thresholds or rates for attributes produced by the federate.
notes: The repo preserves and exposes update-rate metadata and time-state helpers, but broader update-condition semantics remain only partially implemented and harmonized.

requirement_text: Federates that use time management shall conform to the standard time-regulating, time-constrained, lookahead, and time-advance responsibilities.
notes: Time-management responsibilities are strongly represented, but the imported framework statement is broader than the currently isolated proof envelope.

requirement_text: Federates shall be able to vary update conditions described in their SOMs.
notes: The repo preserves and exposes update-rate metadata and time-state helpers, but broader update-condition semantics remain only partially implemented and harmonized.

requirement_text: A federate shall manage local time in a way that permits coordinated data exchange with the federation.
notes: The repo has substantial time-coordination coverage, but the imported framework time statements are broader than the current direct harmonized slices.

requirement_text: Federates shall adhere to the time management approach selected by the federation design.
notes: The repo has substantial time-coordination coverage, but the imported framework time statements are broader than the current direct harmonized slices.

requirement_text: Federates shall manage local behavior so that their use of HLA services remains consistent with the standard rules and the federation object model.
notes: The repo exercises core runtime compliance obligations, but the full federate-local conformance statement remains broader than the current direct proof set.

requirement_text: Federates shall manage local time in a manner compatible with federation time coordination.
notes: The repo has substantial time-coordination coverage, but the imported framework time statements are broader than the current direct harmonized slices.

requirement_text: Project rationale documentation shall preserve traceability from implementation choices to the framework rule rationale where a design decision depends on HLA rule intent.
notes: The repo preserves source-policy and architecture context, but it does not yet maintain a dedicated rationale-to-design-decision trace for every relevant framework choice.

requirement_text: Bibliographic references shall be recorded as non-normative source context and not treated as implementation requirements unless referenced normatively elsewhere.
notes: The source-documents policy now explicitly treats bibliographic and archive-reference material as non-normative source context unless cited normatively elsewhere.
